extraction.one_line_summary: GANNA Filippo won by 1:41 over Bissegger, while KUSS Sepp shockingly defended red by 40 seconds over Soler in dead-flat TT.
tactical_patterns: GANNA caught his 4-minute man before 16km mark, revealing unprecedented pacing aggression that emptied four riders unusually early.
KUSS defended red on pan-flat TT against Soler by 40 seconds, forcing recalibration of pure-climber TT capabilities.
AYUSO placed seventh among pure climbers on flat TT, signaling hidden TT strength often overlooked in GC calculations.
EVENEPOEL finished 16 seconds behind Ganna despite TT specialty, suggesting form decline or tactical pacing conservation for mountains.

rider_signals.KUSS Sepp: positive: Held red by 40s over Soler on dead-flat TT despite being pure climber, contradicts assumed TT weakness
rider_signals.GANNA Filippo: positive: 1:20 faster than Bissegger while catching 4-minute man early reveals unsustainable-seeming pacing that actually held
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: negative: Only 16s faster than climbing specialist Kuss on flat TT raises questions about peak form
rider_signals.AYUSO Juan: positive: Seventh place among pure climbers on flat TT suggests undertrained TT ability could swing future GC battles
rider_signals.SOLER Marc: negative: Lost red by only 26 seconds but couldn't capitalize on Kuss's climber profile on flat terrain
rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: neutral: 20 seconds slower than Evenepoel but 1:36 down on GC suggests tempo-controlled effort preserving energy
